name: CogVLM-17B
license: Apache-2.0 (code); CogVLM Model License for weights (non-commercial use / commercial by agreement)
archDisplay: Dense dual-tower vision-language Transformer (trainable visual expert modules)
note: Open VLM (10B vision expert + 7B Vicuna-based LM) with SOTA-level results on 10 cross-modal benchmarks at 490x490 resolution.

results.notes: Ranked 2nd overall, surpassing/matching PaLI-X 55B [Corrected during full-corpus audit: Directly extracted the full paper PDF text (via pdftotext) rather than relying on a web summary. The paper's actual data tables (Table 2, VQA benchmarks, test-dev split, and Table 3, generalist section, 'single task' row) both report CogVLM's VQAv2 score as 84.7. The figure 84.38 does not appear in either data table; it appears to have been misread from the numeric labels scattered around Figure 1] [score corrected during round-5 full-corpus accuracy re-verify: 84.38 -> 82.3]
Paper Table 2, VQA benchmarks, test split.

Paper Table 2, VQA benchmarks, val split. Slightly exceeds the taxonomy's typical OK-VQA range (max 61) but is verbatim as published (the paper's own comparison table shows PaLI-X-55B scoring 66.1 on the same benchmark).

From a later-revised comparison table in the same paper (arXiv:2311.03079, updated version) for the 'CogVLM-Chat / Vicuna-7B' checkpoint, i.e. the same cogvlm-chat-hf model cataloged here. MMMU/MathVista postdate the Nov-2023 original release, confirming this table reflects a later official update to the paper.
Same later-revised comparison table, CogVLM-Chat/Vicuna-7B row.
Same later-revised comparison table; exact split (dev/test, en/cn) not specified in the compact comparison table, so mapped to the generic mmbench slug.
Same later-revised comparison table (labeled 'SEED'); marginally above the taxonomy's typical range (max 72) but verbatim as published.

Zero-shot CIDEr on Flickr30K Karpathy-test split, Table 1 of the CogVLM paper (arXiv:2311.03079). Paper text explicitly states: 'On the Flickr benchmark, our model achieves a SOTA score of 94.9 surpassing the concurrently released Qwen-VL model by 9.1 points' — consistent with the catalog's existing Qwen-VL flickr30k entry of 85.8 (94.9 - 85.8 = 9.1), cross-confirming both numbers come from the same comparison table.

Table 1 of the CogVLM paper (arXiv:2311.03079), 'CogVLM (ours)' row, COCO Karpathy-test split, CIDEr metric (148.7) — finetuned checkpoint. This is the same table/row already used elsewhere in the file for the existing flickr30k=94.9 entry (both numbers come from the same Table 1 row: NoCaps val/test, Flickr Karpathy 94.9, COCO Karpathy 148.7, TextCaps 144.9), so it is attributed to the cataloged model on the same basis as that pre-existing entry. Not previously tracked under the coco-caption slug.

verificationNotes: Corrected releaseDate from the unconfirmed exact day 2023-10-05 to month-precision 2023-10: GitHub commit history (checked via API) shows the earliest real commits Sept 28-Oct 9, 2023 with no activity specifically on Oct 5; independent Chinese-language press coverage (Jiqizhixin, Tencent News) appeared Oct 12-15, 2023; the arXiv paper wasn't submitted until Nov 6, 2023 and HF weights uploaded Nov 16, 2023. Params (17B = ~10B vision expert + 7B Vicuna LM), Apache-2.0 code license + separate Model License, and INT4 VRAM ~11GB on RTX 3090 all confirmed verbatim in README. VQAv2 84.38 / GQA 82.87 corroborated by an independent web search but I could not directly open the full PDF (10MB size limit) to see the table myself, so treat with slightly lower confidence than other rows. Removed vramFp16Gb (record's 29GB) since README only gives system-level guidance (1xA100-80G or 2xRTX3090-24G) and I found no single verified per-model FP16 VRAM figure to corroborate 29GB specifically.
